notes.author: Dr. Jessica Tanaka
notes.preview: 4-week post-discharge follow-up. Excellent early response: NG tube removed 4/15 with normalized swallow, weight up 9.4 kg from admission nadir (44.8 kg today), albumin 3.4, prealbumin 28 (normal), CK 218 (near-normal), dsDNA 16 (down), complements near-normal, UPC 0.15 (normal — renal involvement resolved), pancytopenia resolved. Patient walking with cane. PHQ-9 6 (mild). Medicaid approved 4/8/2027. Continue MMF 1.5g BID, prednisone taper, HCQ.

notes.author: Dr. Sarah Beckman / Dr. Maria Castro / Dr. David Park / co-signed by Dr. Tanaka
notes.preview: 11-day admission discharge summary. Final dx: SLE with inflammatory myositis (overlap PM/DM features, MSA-negative) + severe oropharyngeal dysphagia + kwashiorkor-pattern PEM + early lupus nephritis (resolving) + pancytopenia + Hashimoto's. Treatment: pulse methylpred 1g x 3 (HD8-10) → prednisone 40 mg with planned taper; MMF advanced to 1.5g BID; HCQ continued; nutritional rehabilitation with NG tube ramped to 75 mL/hr by HD10; swallow studies improving. Discharge weight 39.4 kg (+4 from admission). Comprehensive outpatient follow-up arranged with Medicaid coverage.

notes.author: Dr. David Park / Dr. Maria Castro / Dr. Sarah Beckman
notes.preview: HD8 synthesis note integrating muscle biopsy (active inflammatory myopathy with overlap PM/DM features), MSA panel (all negative), skin biopsy (mixed kwashiorkor + SLE pattern), echo (normal LV, trace pericardial effusion), MBSS (severe oropharyngeal dysphagia with silent aspiration), EMG (active inflammatory myopathy). UNIFYING DIAGNOSIS: SLE → myositis → dysphagia → starvation → kwashiorkor. Treatment response evident. Plan: initiate pulse methylprednisolone today + continue MMF + nutrition advance.

notes.author: Dr. Vivian Chen / Dr. Sarah Lo
notes.preview: HD6. Bedside derm consult + second skin biopsy from abdominal 'flaky paint' lesion. Pathology shows interface/vacuolar dermatitis with epidermal cytoplasmic vacuolation, weakly positive lupus band test, absent dermal mucin. Interpretation: MIXED process — kwashiorkor/nutritional dermatosis layered on underlying SLE-associated cutaneous disease. 'Flag sign' hair confirmed. Recommends nutrition-first approach; expect improvement with refeeding.

notes.author: Dr. Rebecca Yang / Dr. Marc Albertini
notes.preview: HD5. Open biopsy of right vastus lateralis (bedside, US-guided). Path final 3/22: ACTIVE INFLAMMATORY MYOPATHY with prominent CD8+ T-cell endomysial infiltrate invading non-necrotic fibers (PM-like), perifascicular atrophy in ~30% of fascicles (DM-like), diffuse MHC class I upregulation, C5b-9 sarcolemmal deposition perifascicular. NO rimmed vacuoles, normal sarcoglycan. Interpretation: SLE-ASSOCIATED INFLAMMATORY MYOSITIS WITH OVERLAP DM FEATURES.

notes.author: Dr. Rebecca Yang
notes.preview: HD4. Bedside EMG/NCS. NCS: normal (no neuropathy). EMG: ACTIVE INFLAMMATORY MYOPATHY pattern — proximal-predominant fibrillations 2-3+, positive sharp waves, small short polyphasic MUPs with early recruitment, paraspinal involvement. RNS rules out MG. Differential argues for inflammatory (vs toxic, endocrine, hereditary, steroid). RIGHT VASTUS LATERALIS identified as biopsy target.

notes.author: Eun-Ji Lee, RD, CSP, CNSC
notes.preview: HD4. Comprehensive nutrition assessment confirming SEVERE PROTEIN-CALORIE MALNUTRITION (kwashiorkor pattern): albumin 1.6, prealbumin 6, anasarca, flaky-paint dermatosis, flag-sign hair, Beau's lines, severe muscle wasting on CT, 30.3% weight loss. HIGH REFEEDING-SYNDROME RISK per NICE criteria. Detailed refeeding plan: NG Nutren 1.0 starting 360 kcal/d (10 kcal/kg) with 5 kcal/kg daily advance, thiamine before each carb load, q6h electrolytes.

notes.author: Dr. Hannah Park / Dr. Jessica Tanaka
notes.preview: HD3 consult by Dr. Tanaka's team (continuity of care). Confirms working diagnosis: ACTIVE SLE + inflammatory myositis + kwashiorkor-pattern PEM. Articulates unifying causal chain. Plan: hold pulse steroids pending biopsy + MSA results + clean infection screen; restart MMF via NG; await biopsy results before deciding pulse vs. high-dose oral + possible IVIG depending on PM vs DM features.

notes.author: Carolyn Wu, MS, CCC-SLP / Dr. Jordan Tate
notes.preview: HD3 repeat MBSS. SEVERE oropharyngeal dysphagia with aspiration of thin liquids (silent aspiration on 1 of 3 swallows), aspiration with chewing of soft solid, mild nasal regurgitation. NPO with NG tube recommended. Pattern is neuromuscular/myopathic.

notes.author: Dr. David Park / Dr. Maria Castro / Dr. Sarah Beckman
notes.preview: Admission H&P for 23F with active SLE + new inflammatory myositis + severe oropharyngeal dysphagia presenting with multi-system deterioration: BMI 15.7 (30.3% weight loss), anasarca, 'flag sign' hair, kwashiorkor-pattern scaling dermatosis, proximal weakness 2-3/5, dysphonia, oral ulcers. Labs: Hgb 7.8, WBC 2.30, albumin 1.6, prealbumin 6, CK 2360, anti-dsDNA 48, C3 58, C4 5, electrolyte derangements with refeeding risk. Working unifying dx: SLE -> myositis -> dysphagia -> starvation -> kwashiorkor.

notes.author: Dr. Sarah Beckman / Dr. Maria Castro
notes.preview: 4-day admission for myositis/dysphagia workup ending in AMA discharge. MBSS 2/9 showed mild oropharyngeal dysphagia with laryngeal penetration of thin liquids; nectar-thick/pureed diet with chin-tuck. Skin biopsy taken 2/10 (pending). Multidisciplinary consults: rheum, neuro, GI, ID, derm, nutrition. EMG and muscle biopsy scheduled but NOT done due to AMA discharge.

notes.author: Dr. James Tran
notes.preview: 23F with active SLE on HCQ/MMF/prednisone presenting with new oropharyngeal dysphagia + proximal weakness + voice change + 18.9% weight loss (now 41.2 kg). CK 1840, aldolase 22, anti-dsDNA 38, low complements, mild proteinuria, oral ulcer. Diagnosis: SLE flare with new inflammatory myositis.

notes.author: Dr. Andre Williams
notes.preview: 6-week PCP follow-up. SLE responding well to MMF + HCQ + prednisone taper. Joint pain much better, rash mostly resolved, weight up to 47.2 kg, mood improved. NEW SYMPTOM: occasional bolus dysphagia with dry foods x 2-3 weeks. Bedside swallow screen equivocal. Ordered CK / aldolase / LDH.

notes.author: Dr. Jessica Tanaka
notes.preview: 5-day post-ED urgent re-evaluation. Modest clinical improvement on prednisone 30 mg + restarted HCQ. Added mycophenolate 500 mg BID. Contraception counseling. Strong adherence and access plan with social work.

notes.author: Dr. Priya Shah
notes.preview: 23-year-old woman with UCTD/probable early SLE and Hashimoto's thyroiditis presenting with worsening fatigue, joint pain, weight loss, intermittent rash. Weight 43.8 kg (down 7 kg / 13.8% from baseline). Anti-dsDNA now positive, complements low, mild proteinuria, tri-lineage cytopenia. Recommended admission; patient declined.

notes.author: Dr. Andre Williams
notes.preview: 23-year-old woman with UCTD off HCQ for 5 weeks. Joint pain markedly worse, new photosensitive rash, 4.4 kg weight loss, new mild cytopenia, low albumin, elevated ESR/CRP, PHQ-9 11. Bridged HCQ via low-cost cash pay.

notes.author: Walgreens Patterson Park / Esperanza Health staff
notes.preview: HCQ refill could not be filled — manufacturer PAP renewal denied. Patient out of HCQ since ~8/12/2026.

notes.author: Diana Mercado, LCSW
notes.preview: Medicaid application DENIED 5/29/2026; appeal filed today. PAP applications submitted. Food insecurity worsening. Mental health concerns emerging.

notes.author: Dr. Jessica Tanaka
notes.preview: First specialist evaluation. Assessment: undifferentiated CTD, possible early SLE; co-existing Hashimoto's. Continue HCQ, add low-dose prednisone taper. Hand X-rays show non-erosive synovitis.

notes.author: Dr. Manuel Reyes
notes.preview: 2-week follow-up. Send-out labs returned: anti-Sm, anti-dsDNA, anti-Ro/La, anti-CCP all negative; hep B/C/HIV negative; parvovirus IgM negative; TPO antibody positive (Hashimoto's confirmed).

notes.author: Dr. Manuel Reyes
notes.preview: 22-year-old Spanish-speaking woman presenting with 6-month history of progressive joint pain, fatigue, and hair loss. ANA positive 1:80. Started empirically on hydroxychloroquine and levothyroxine pending rheumatology referral.
